ogTitle: Lonesome Creek vs Long Pocket: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lonesome Creek and Long Pocket.
heading: Lonesome Creek vs Long Pocket — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lonesome Creek, Queensland and Long Pocket, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Long Pocket has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Lonesome Creek (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lonesome Creek or Long Pocket safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lonesome Creek has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Long Pocket scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lonesome Creek scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Long Pocket scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lonesome Creek or Long Pocket?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Long Pocket has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
